Code Status: Full code - Chest compressions / Intubate / Full Treatment
Humerus head fracture, left, closed, initial encounter Yes
Patient had a mechanical fall suffering a left shoulder fracture. She is traveling alone and was having difficulty doing
her ADL's independently. She had pain control (doesn't like narcotics) and will go home with follow up with an
She was baseline otherwise with no infection.
Arrangements were made for her to have a traveling nurse to assist her back to the mainland with an orthopedic
evaluation as soon as possible and to a STR facility.
Patients Allergies: Patient has no known allergies.

Unspecified displaced fracture of surgical neck of left humerus, subsequent encounter for fracture with reutine
On cruise in Hawaii, tripped and fell onboard on 12/28/25. Suffered Humerus head fracture, left, closed Pathologic fracture
of the left proximal humerus due to osteoporosis.Inpatient hospital 12/28/25-1/4/26 and then flown from Hawaii to
Ridgecrest Post acute on 1/4/26-1/12/28 and then to AHC Scottsdale for ongoing skilled care
Pain control; tylenol 500mg QHS and Q6H prn, oxy 5mg Q6 hr pm
PU NILOOFAR DEHGHAN,Orthopedic Surgery on 2/11/26 s/p Left Reverse Shoulder Arthroplasty on 1/26/26
The sutures were removed during the visit. The patient no longer requires the sling but may use it for comfort or in
crowded settings to protect the shoulder.
She is advised to avoid external rotation beyond neutral for six weeks following surgery. She is encouraged to move her
shoulder as tolerated within the prescribed parameters and to move her wrist and elbow freely.
She may lift items equivalent to the weight of a coffee cup or plate of food with two hands. She is cleared to shower,
allowing soap and water to run over the incision, and instructed to pat the area dry without rubbing.
The patient is informed that she can sleep In any position that is comfortable, with most patients preferring a recliner or
pillow. She is advised to place a thin pillow between her torso and arm for additional support If sleeping on her back,
From an orthopedic standpoint, the patient is deemed safe to recover at home. However, the decision regarding discharge
from the rehabilitation facility will be made collectively by the facility staff, considering her overall recovery and ability to
manage activities of daily living. Follow-up with the surgeon is scheduled in approximately one month.
2/27/26 DC planning for next Thursday: Patient tolerating therapy. increased ROM w/o pain +stiffness of Left shoulder
weekness due to Reverse Total Shoulder Arthroplasty on 1/26/26
Shoulder precautions per Ortho MD FU 2/11/26 Next FU 3/12/26

Pt is a 76 y/o female hospitalized in Kaul on 12/28 after falling while on a cruise ship in Hawaii, Pt dx with L
communted fx of humerus and also inferior glanoid rim. Pt placed in sling and to be NWB LUE. Pt also reports pain In
L knee as "knee buckled" leading to the GLF. Imaging is negative for dis/ocation/fx. Pt reports f/u ortho SX consult OR
PMHx: Glaucoma, hypothyroldism, HTN, interstitial lung disease.
Prior Living Environment = Patient resided in private residence.
Prior Living Description: Pt lives alone in SLH with 3 STE, IND with ADLs and IADLs. IND with amb.
Prior Cognitive Assistance = No supervision required
Transition/Discharge Plan = Patient to live alone in private residence.
Assessment and Summary of Skilled Services
Skilled Interventions Provided: Interventions of focus this reporting period that required the skills of a therapist
included assessment of bed mobility. transfers, gait, L shoulder ROM.
Assessment and Summary of Skilled Services
Progress & Response to Tx: Patient exhibits ability to participate in tasks of increased complexity to further enhance
PT/Asst. Supervision: Skilled services provided by therapist this reporting period, as well as with assistant.
Team Communication ; Collaboration: Functional skills reviewed with team members.
Remaining Impairments: decreased ROM, decreased functional capacity and pain.
Reason for Skilled Services: In order to facilitate return to PLOF. patient requires continued PT services in order to
Increase LUE ROM and strength and increase functional activity tolerance in order to enhance patient's quality of life
by improving ability to return to prior level of functional abilities.
Level of Skilled Services: The recommended level of skilled therapy services is required due to the following
complexities and comorbidities that impact treatment: Age.
Potential for Achieving Rehab Goals: Patient demonstrates excellent rehab potential as evidenced by ability to follow
2-step directions, ability to participate in tasks of increased complexity, active participation in skilled treatment and
motivated to return to prior level of living.
Focus of Plan of Treatment = Restoration,Compensation,Adaptation
Electronically signed by Allison Dudzik, PT, DPT

fracture, TSA pending, (+) L knee pain/buckling (no acute injuries)
PMHx: glaucoma, hypothyroldism, HTN, interstitial lung disease, osteorporosis, arthritis
Prior Living Environment = Patient resided in private residence.
Prior Living Description: Lives alone SLH, 3 steps to enter at front. but typically enters from garage, regular bed, has
4WW but wasn't using It, has newly acquired herniwalker, use of SPC on etairs (difficulty with curbs/steps) and in
community otherwise wasn't using AD for mobility, WIS no grab bars, no chair, stationary shower head, doesn't wear
socks, elevated tollets, uses pony wall next to toilet to assist with transfers, does not have a bldet, no family or
Prior Cognitive Assistance = No supervision required
Transition/Discharge Plan = Patient to live at home w/support/(A) from others.
Skilled Interventions Provided: ADLs, with attempts at training for transfers, toileting. upper/lower body dressing, G/H,
shower/bathing. WC mobility and gait/ambulation. Attempts at ther-act and ther-ex to increase overall strength,
balance and endurance In prep for improved carryover with functional tasks
Progress & Response to Tx: Patient is compliant with Plan Of Treatment and Patient is making consistent progress
towards reaching ST and LT goals.
OT/Asst. Supervision : Therapist participated in direct patient care during this reporting period.
Team Communication / Collaboration: Treatment results communicated to Interdisicplinary Team,
Remaining Impairments: decreased static/dynamic balance, decreased functional capacity, pain and strength
Reason for Skilled Services: Patient requires skilled OT services to assess safety and independence with ADLs,
assess safety and independence with self-care and functional tasks of choice, develop and instruct in exercise
program. develop and instruct on adaptation techniques, develop and instruct on compensatory strategies, facilitate
dynamic standing balance, facilitate independence with ADLs, improve motor control/tone in upper extremity. Improve
rehab potential, increase functional activity tolerance, increase Independence with ADLs, increase safety awareness.
maximize Independence with ADLs, maximize rehab potential. provision of modalities and strengthening and
provision of pain management techniques in order to enhance patient's quality of life by improving ability to be able to
return to prior level of living.
Level of Skilled Services: The recommended level of skilled therapy services is required due to the following
complexities and comorbidities that impact treatment: High level prior level of function, Pain severity level and Strong
motivation to achieve prior level of function.
Potential for Achieving Rehab Goals: Patient demonstrates good rehab potential as evidericed by ability to follow
multi-step directions, ability to self correct, motivated to participate, stable medical condition and Intreased functional
Focus of Plan of Treatment = Restoration, Compensation
